create_context.ms:
Create a Dear PyGui context, initializing internal state and resources.

Must be called first before using any other Dear PyGui controls or windows.

Effectively 'boots' Dear PyGui, preparing for subsequent widget and window creation.

create_fps_matrix.ms:
Create a first-person shooter (FPS) style transformation matrix, usually used for 3D rendering or camera control.

Can be used with drawing nodes or 3D rendering controls to implement free-view camera movement.

Camera position (eye position), typically [x, y, z]
args.pitch (float): Up/down rotation (pitch) in radians. Positive looks up, negative looks down.
args.yaw (float): Left/right rotation (yaw) in radians. Positive turns right, negative turns left.

create_lookat_matrix.ms:
Create a LookAt matrix, commonly used for 3D rendering or camera control.

Transforms world coordinates to camera coordinates, making the camera face a specified target.

args.up (Union[List[float], Tuple[float, ...]: Camera up vector [x, y, z], usually [0, 1, 0] for Y-up, defines the camera's 'roll' direction

create_orthographic_matrix.ms:
Create an orthographic matrix for orthographic projection in 3D rendering.

Orthographic projection does not apply perspective scaling; object size is independent of depth. Useful for CAD, charts, or certain 2D/3D visualization.

args.zNear (float): Near clipping plane, closest distance the camera can see
args.zFar (float): Far clipping plane, farthest distance the camera can see

create_perspective_matrix.ms:
Create a perspective matrix for perspective projection in 3D rendering.

Objects closer to the camera appear larger, farther objects smaller.

args.fov (float): Field of view in radians, controls visible angle. Larger values = wider view
args.aspect (float): Aspect ratio (width ÷ height) to maintain correct projection proportions

create_rotation_matrix.ms:
Create a rotation matrix, used to rotate 3D objects or layers.

Can be applied directly to drawing nodes or 3D rendering controls.

args.angle (float): Rotation angle in radians. Positive follows right-hand rule.
Rotation axis vector [x, y, z]. Must be non-zero, will be normalized.

create_scale_matrix.ms:
Create a scale matrix to scale layers or 3D objects.

Can be applied directly to drawing nodes or 3D rendering controls.

args.scales (Union[List[float], Tuple[float,: Scaling factors [sx, sy, sz] for X, Y, Z axes. Example: [2.0, 1.0, 0.5] doubles X, keeps Y, halves Z.

create_translation_matrix.ms:
Create a translation matrix to move layers or 3D objects.

Can be applied directly to drawing nodes or 3D rendering controls.

args.translation (Union[List[float], Tuple[fl: Translation vector [tx, ty, tz] along X, Y, Z axes. Example: [1.0, 0.0, -2.0] moves right by 1, no Y change, into screen by 2.

create_viewport.ms:
Create a viewport, the main window for Dear PyGui rendering.

The viewport is the 'canvas' of the application. All windows, widgets, and drawings are displayed on it.

An application needs at least one viewport to display content.

args.small_icon (str, optional): Small icon path (.ico for Windows, .ico/.png for Mac)
args.large_icon (str, optional): Large icon path (.ico for Windows, .ico/.png for Mac)
